commands.review_summary:
The branch modifies seven Settings/manifest files to replace the Settings split view, improve missing-app/install copy, and switch Telecrawl's primary macOS bundle identifier.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence reproduction path was established in this review. This is a PR review rather than an issue repro, and I did not run the packaged macOS UI; the body provides copied runtime output but not the visible screenshots.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human review because it is a draft stacked UI PR with insufficient uploaded visual proof; there is no narrow repair for automation to apply.

target.title: Fix CrawlBar settings sidebar UI bugs
target.body:
# Human intent

Josh wants PR #14 kept as a sequence of small stacked reviewable slices. This follow-up fixes the concrete UI bugs found while clicking around the crawler suggestions branch: the Settings sidebar should stay readable, the sidebar toggle should not move around, the window must not resize after hiding and reopening the sidebar, install buttons should say when they use Homebrew, missing source apps should be described as missing apps rather than missing crawlers, and Telegram should be detected with the local macOS Telegram bundle ID.

# Summary

- replace the unstable Settings `NavigationSplitView` sidebar with a small fixed-width settings shell so sidebar hide/show does not resize the window
- keep sidebar row selection explicit and accessible after removing split-view selection binding
- make source-app-backed More Crawlers copy say `No <app> app found`
- make missing-source-app detail copy say `No <app> app was found on this Mac`
- label Homebrew install actions as `Install with Homebrew`
- use `ru.keepcoder.Telegram` for the Telegram app icon/detection while preserving the existing alternative suggestion bundle ID

# Stack

Base: #14 (`codex/crawlbar-crawler-suggestions`)

This PR intentionally does not touch #14's manifest classification model, menu grouping rules, packaging, launch activation policy, CLI contracts, or crawler command behavior.

# Proof

Head checked: `877fdc9`

```text
$ git diff --check origin/codex/crawlbar-crawler-suggestions..HEAD
ok

$ swift build
Build complete!

$ swift run crawlbar-selftest
crawlbar selftest ok

$ Scripts/package_app.sh
<repo>/dist/CrawlBar.app

$ codesign --verify --deep --strict --verbose=2 dist/CrawlBar.app
dist/CrawlBar.app: valid on disk
dist/CrawlBar.app: satisfies its Designated Requirement

$ dist/CrawlBar.app/Contents/Helpers/crawlbar config validate
ok
```

Packaged-app sidebar proof from cropped local window captures:

```text
visible after launch:       1120x874
hidden after toggle:        1120x874
visible after second toggle: 1120x874
```

AX selection proof for the Slack row:

```text
AXButton | Slack (slacrawl), Disabled, No Slack app found
press=0
```

The local screenshots were inspected before opening this PR, but they are not uploaded here because Josh asked to approve local media before shipping it. No raw private crawler data, message bodies, contacts, tokens, endpoints, phone numbers, or account-specific source content is included in this proof.

# AI Slop below

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels
target.author: joshp123
